2	  "body": "**Preferred term labels:**\r\n- [x] antimesosalpinx #3420 \r\n\r\n- [ ] mesosalpinx epithelium\r\n- [ ] antimesosalpinx epithelium\r\n\r\n- [ ] mesosalpinx muscularus\r\n- [ ] antimesosalpinx muscularus\r\n\r\n**Synonyms**\r\nantimesosalpinx = posterior free edge of mesosalpinx\r\n\r\n**Definition (free text, please give PubMed ID)**\r\nantimesosalpinx is the posterior free edge of the mesosalpinx.\r\n\r\nmesosalpinx epithelium is composed of simple squamous epithelium that forms the outer layer of the mesosalpinx.\r\n\r\nmesosalpinx muscularus The muscular layer (myosalpinx or tunica muscularis) consists primarily of an inner layer of circularly arranged muscle fibers covered by an outer layer of longitudinally oriented fibers. These longitudinally arranged fibers are continuous with the smooth muscle present in the mesosalpinx.\r\n\r\n**possible source of images for entries**\r\nhttps://www.pathologyoutlines.com/topic/fallopiantubesnormal.html\r\n\r\n**Parent term (use https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/uberon)**\r\nmesosalpinx UBERON:0012331 http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0012331\r\n\r\n**Your nano-attribution (ORCID)**\r\n0000-0001-7655-4833\r\n\r\n\r\n",

7	        "login": "aleixpuigb"
8	      },
9	      "authorAssociation": "MEMBER",
10	      "body": "> mesosalpinx muscularus\r\n\r\nI suspect that this structure might be referring to the [myosalpinx](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/agricultural-and-biological-sciences/mesosalpinx#:~:text=Myosalpinx,al.%2C%202015).), or tunica muscularis. If it is the case, it is a separate layer than the mesosalpinx, and therefore it wouldn't need a term for antimesosalpinx muscularis. I don't think that the mesosalpinx have a muscular layer.",

25	      "body": "@aleixpuigb from fallopian tube experts for ontology placement: \r\nBegin forwarded message:\r\n\r\nFrom: \"Nordgren, C. Erik\" <nordgren@sas.upenn.edu>\r\nSubject: Re: Fallopian tube cell type ontology\r\nDate: November 26, 2024 at 10:11:13 AM EST\r\nTo: \"Quardokus, Ellen M\" <ellenmq@iu.edu>, Aleix Puig <aleix@ebi.ac.uk>\r\nCc: \"Fisher, Stephen A\" <safisher@sas.upenn.edu>, Kate O'Neill <Kathleen.O'neill2@pennmedicine.upenn.edu>, \"Kuang, Da\" <kuangda@seas.upenn.edu>\r\n\r\nHi Ellen & Aleix,\r\n\r\nHaving consulted further with Kate, I wanted to get back to you with more specificity on the placement of the new functional tissue unit ontological terms we need added for Fallopian tube.\r\n\r\nSo, under \"muscle layer of oviduct\", should be placed:\r\nmesosalpinx muscularus\r\nantimesosalpinx muscularus\r\n\r\nAnd under \"mucosa of fallopian tube\", should be placed:\r\nmesosalpinx epithelium\r\nantimesosalpinx epithelium\r\nAll of these terms would of course apply to both the left and right organs.\r\nDoes that give you what you need, in order to have these new terms added and published?\r\nErik\r\n",

If it is the case, it is a separate layer than the mesosalpinx, and therefore it wouldn't need a term for antimesosalpinx muscularis. 77	      "body": "After discussing with SMEs, we need 8 new terms:\n- [ ] mesosalpinx epithelium\n- [ ] antimesosalpinx epithelium\n- [ ] mesosalpinx muscularus\n- [ ] antimesosalpinx muscularus\n- [ ] superior epithelium\n- [ ] inferior epithelium\n- [ ] superior muscularus\n- [ ] inferior muscularus\n\nThe mesosalpinx, antimesosalping, superior and inferior only makes reference to the region (polarity) in the muscular layer or epithelium of the fallopian tube, but they are not part of the mesosalpinx or antimesosalpinx.",

id: UBERON:0012331
name: mesosalpinx
def: "A serous layer that is part of the peritoneal lining of the abdominal cavity in higher vertebrates, specifically the portion of the broad ligament that stretches from the uterine (fallopian) tube to the ovary. format-version: 1.2
subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."
subsetdef: common_anatomy "Terms applicable across life, not restricted to Metazoa"
subsetdef: cumbo "CUMBO"
subsetdef: cyclostome_subset "cyclostome structures"
subsetdef: defined_by_cytoarchitecture "brain region defined by cyto-architecture"
subsetdef: defined_by_ordinal_series "classes that are defined by relative position counting from first in a series of elements along an axis in an individual organism rather than by strict homology"
subsetdef: developmental_classification "developmental_classification"
subsetdef: dubious_grouping "Indicates that subclasses in AOs may be inappropriately linked"
subsetdef: early_development "A class that represents an early developmental structure, like a blastocyst. This part of the ontology is undergoing review to remove inappropriate grouping classes."
subsetdef: efo_slim "EFO slim"
subsetdef: emapa_ehdaa2 "derived from the union of EHDAA2 and EMAPA - still to be checked"
subsetdef: feed_aligned "subset of classes that have logical defs text defs and synonyms aligned with FEED"
subsetdef: functional_classification "functional_classification"
subsetdef: grouping_class "Subset consisting of classes creating for grouping purposes"
subsetdef: homology_grouping "A grouping class that depends on an assumption of homology between subclasses"
subsetdef: human_reference_atlas ""
subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""
subsetdef: major_organ "somewhat fuzzy grouping for analysis purposes, currently composed of something like: liver, heart, skeletal, kidney, bladder, brain, skin, mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestines, trachea nose, lungs, brain, spinal cord,  peripheral nerves, kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra, gonads"
subsetdef: metazoa_core "core classes typically found across metazoa. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new metazoan AO. Note this subset is not yet fully fleshed out"
subsetdef: non_informative "abstract class brought in to group ontology classes but not informative"
subsetdef: organ_slim "organs, excluding individual muscles and skeletal elements"
subsetdef: pheno_slim "Phenotype slim"
subsetdef: phenotype_rcn "A subset specifically created for the 2012 Phenotype RCN meeting. Includes some human-specific terms that may eventually be removed when they are adequately represented with part of relationships in FMA"
subsetdef: uberon_slim "Uberon slim - subset that excludes obscure terms and deep compositional terms"
subsetdef: unverified_taxonomic_grouping "Indicates that the class encompasses classes from other AOs that have not been verified"
subsetdef: upper_level "abstract upper-level terms not directly useful for analysis"
subsetdef: vertebrate_core "core classes typically found across vertebrates. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new vertebrate AO"
synonymtypedef: BRAIN_NAME_ABV "brain name abbreviations (drosophila)"
synonymtypedef: COMPARATIVE_PREFERRED "preferred term in the context of comparative anatomy"
synonymtypedef: DEPRECATED "A historic synonym, no longer encouraged"
synonymtypedef: DEVELOPMENTAL "developmental term"
synonymtypedef: HOMOLOGY "a synonym made on the basis of a possibly homologous structure in another species"
synonymtypedef: HUMAN_PREFERRED "preferred term when talking about an instance of this class in Homo sapiens"
synonymtypedef: INCONSISTENT "indicates that a synonym is used in an inconsistent or confusing way, typically between species"
synonymtypedef: MISSPELLING "a mis-spelling that is in common use and thus recorded"
synonymtypedef: NON_AMNIOTE "synonym that is inappropriate for amniotes"
synonymtypedef: NON_MAMMAL "synonym that is inappropriate for mammals"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003000 "abbreviation"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003002 "dubious synonym"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003004 "plural form"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003011 "latin term"
synonymtypedef: PENDING_REVIEW "expert consultation and attribution required"
synonymtypedef: SENSU "taxonomic disambiguation"
synonymtypedef: SYSTEMATIC "a systematic synonym used as the base name for design patterns"

2. **Axis** — what does "superior / inferior" refer to here? The cross-sectional polarity (e.g. dorsal / ventral aspects of the tubal wall) or the longitudinal axis of the tube (e.g. infundibular vs. uterine end)?
3. **Definitions and references** — short free-text definitions plus a citation (ideally a PMID/DOI).
4. **Parent terms** — same as the four above (mucosa of fallopian tube / muscle layer of oviduct), or different?

Once those are confirmed I'm happy to add the remaining four in a follow-up PR.

# Add fallopian tube layer regional terms (issue #3414)

Addresses [issue #3414](https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3414). Adds four new UBERON terms representing the regional/polarity subdivisions of the fallopian tube's mucosa and muscle layer.

## New terms

| ID | Label | `is_a` parent | `adjacent_to` |
|---|---|---|---|
| UBERON:8600124 | mesosalpinx mucosa of fallopian tube | UBERON:0005048 mucosa of fallopian tube | UBERON:0012331 mesosalpinx |
| UBERON:8600125 | antimesosalpinx mucosa of fallopian tube | UBERON:0005048 mucosa of fallopian tube | UBERON:8600117 antimesosalpinx |
| UBERON:8600126 | mesosalpinx muscularis of fallopian tube | UBERON:0006642 muscle layer of oviduct | UBERON:0012331 mesosalpinx |
| UBERON:8600127 | antimesosalpinx muscularis of fallopian tube | UBERON:0006642 muscle layer of oviduct | UBERON:8600117 antimesosalpinx |

Each new term has:
- a textual definition,
- the requester-supplied informal label as a `RELATED` synonym ("mesosalpinx epithelium", "mesosalpinx muscularus", etc.),
- two descriptive `EXACT` synonyms,
- `dc-contributor` attributions for the requesters (Ellen Quardokus, Aleix Puig-Barbé),
- a `dcterms-date` timestamp,
- a `term_tracker_item` pointing back to issue #3414,
- `created_by: dragon-ai-agent`.

## Rationale and design decisions

### Placement under fallopian tube layers, not under mesosalpinx

The original issue body described the epithelium term as "the outer layer of the mesosalpinx" (which would make it a serous/mesothelial structure). However, the subsequent SME consultation forwarded from Dr. Erik Nordgren placed the terms under:

- **muscle layer of oviduct** (UBERON:0006642) for the muscularis variants
- **mucosa of fallopian tube** (UBERON:0005048) for the epithelium variants

This was further clarified by @aleixpuigb (2025-02-13):

> The mesosalpinx, antimesosalpinx, superior and inferior only makes reference to the region (polarity) in the muscular layer or epithelium of the fallopian tube, but they are not part of the mesosalpinx or antimesosalpinx.

I followed this later, more authoritative interpretation. Consequently:
- the terms are **not** `part_of` mesosalpinx / antimesosalpinx;
- they are placed `is_a` mucosa-of-fallopian-tube / muscle-layer-of-oviduct;
- the spatial relationship to (anti)mesosalpinx is captured with `adjacent_to`.

### Naming

The issue requested informal labels like "mesosalpinx epithelium" / "mesosalpinx muscularus". I made the primary labels more explicit (e.g. "mesosalpinx mucosa of fallopian tube") because:

1. The expert placement is under the fallopian-tube *mucosa*, not just its epithelium — "mucosa" is the correct parent-aligned label.
2. Bare names like "mesosalpinx epithelium" are easily misread as referring to the mesothelium of the mesosalpinx itself, which is **not** what the SMEs described.
3. "Muscularis" is the standard medical-Latin form; "muscularus" appears to be a typo that propagated through the email thread.

The requester labels are preserved as `RELATED` synonyms so they remain searchable.

### Scope: 4 of the 8 requested terms

The most recent comment lists 8 terms; this PR addresses the 4 mesosalpinx / antimesosalpinx variants only. The "superior / inferior epithelium / muscularis" set has not been added because:

- the request does not specify what "superior / inferior" refers to (cross-sectional polarity? longitudinal axis of the tube?);
- bare labels like "superior epithelium" would clash with many unrelated tissues;
- no definitions or references were supplied for these four.

A request for clarification is included in `ISSUE_COMMENTS.md`; the remaining four can be added in a follow-up PR.

## Self-review checklist

- [x] All new terms have an ID in the conventional UBERON range, contiguous with the most recently used IDs in `src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo` (the latest pre-existing one was UBERON:8600123).
- [x] Each term has a definition with at least one xref.
- [x] Each term has a `dc-contributor` relationship for each named requester (ORCIDs verified against the existing antimesosalpinx term UBERON:8600117 and the issue body).
- [x] Each term has a `dcterms-date` timestamp (2026-05-14T00:44:08Z) and a `term_tracker_item` link to issue #3414.
- [x] Each term has at least one `is_a` parent (which is a real, existing UBERON class).
- [x] The `adjacent_to` (RO:0002220) relationship is used for the spatial link to (anti)mesosalpinx; verified that the typedef is present in `uberon-edit.obo`.
- [x] No relationships point to obsolete terms; parents and `adjacent_to` targets were inspected.
- [x] After check-in, `uberon-edit.obo` was re-serialised with `robot convert -f obo` and re-inspected; all four new stanzas are present and intact.
- [x] The terms were added via the `terms/` checkout/checkin workflow as required by `CLAUDE.md`.

Signed: @dragon-ai-agent
